title: Science Fell in Love, So I Tried to Prove It
altTitles: Rikei ga Koi ni Ochita no de Shoumei shitemita.
description:
Science Fell in Love, So I Tried to Prove It is a romantic comedy anime that follows two brilliant graduate students who attempt to scientifically prove the existence of love through logic, experiments, and mathematical analysis. The story takes place at Saitama University, where researchers dedicate their lives to studying science while completely struggling to understand their own emotions. The anime begins when Ayame Himuro, a highly intelligent and logical scientist, suddenly confesses her feelings to fellow researcher Shinya Yukimura. Although Yukimura is shocked by the confession, he refuses to accept love as a simple emotional phenomenon without scientific evidence. Instead of starting a normal relationship, the two decide to conduct detailed experiments to determine whether their feelings can truly be defined and proven scientifically.Together with their eccentric lab partners, the group performs ridiculous yet surprisingly detailed research involving heart rate measurements, romantic simulations, emotional analysis, data collection, and complex mathematical theories related to attraction and affection. Their scientific approach to romance leads to countless hilarious misunderstandings and awkward situations as they try to calculate emotions that clearly cannot be explained through logic alone. 

Despite the comedy, the anime gradually explores the emotional vulnerability hidden beneath the characters’ intellectual personalities. As Himuro and Yukimura spend more time together, they slowly begin realizing that human emotions are far more complicated than formulas, experiments, or scientific theories. Their journey becomes a charming mix of romance, self-discovery, and emotional growth.The anime combines romance, comedy, slice-of-life storytelling, and educational scientific concepts while presenting relationships in a unique and entertaining way. Many episodes cleverly explain real scientific theories, statistics, and psychological concepts while blending them naturally into romantic comedy scenarios.Science Fell in Love, So I Tried to Prove It became popular for its clever humor, lovable characters, educational elements, and refreshing take on romantic comedy anime. Fans appreciated the series for balancing scientific comedy with genuine emotional moments while creating a fun and memorable story about trying to measure something as unpredictable as love itself.
